question_text: Why does the biconcave shape of a mature red blood cell increase its efficiency in oxygen transport?
visual_description: A schematic showing a spherical cell versus a biconcave disc of equal volume, highlighting larger surface area of the biconcave shape.
subject: Biology

question_text: How does the structure of an artery wall differ from that of a vein to accommodate higher blood pressure?
visual_description: Comparison showing thicker tunica media in artery and valves in vein.
subject: Biology

question_text: Which feature of alveoli simultaneously supports both efficient gas exchange and prevents lung collapse?
visual_description: Cross-section showing thin alveolar wall, capillary, and red blood cell.
subject: Biology

question_text: A person has difficulty seeing distant objects clearly, but near objects are sharp. Which condition does this describe, and where does the image focus relative to the retina?
visual_description: Diagram showing parallel light rays entering an elongated eyeball, converging at a focal point anterior to the retina, resulting in a blurry image on the retina.
subject: Biology

question_text: Under the condition that all 20 billion seedlings survive to maturity, the Green Legacy Initiative would sequester approximately \(4.4 \times 10^{11}\) kg of \(\mathrm{CO_2}\) annually. How does this sequestration compare to Ethiopia's total annual \(\mathrm{CO_2}\) emissions shown in Figure 6.3?
visual_description: Bar chart showing CO2 emissions in million tonnes per year from 1800 to 2019, with rapid increase after 1950 reaching approximately 16 million tonnes by 2019.
subject: Biology

question_text: A graph shows forest disturbance frequency on the x-axis and forest biomass accumulation on the y-axis. As disturbance frequency increases beyond the recovery time, what pattern would the graph show?
visual_description: Graph showing forest biomass on y-axis and disturbance frequency on x-axis. Biomass remains near carrying capacity at low disturbance frequencies, then drops sharply after a threshold where disturbance interval equals recovery time.
subject: Biology

question_text: A student observes that when a piece of copper metal is placed into a silver nitrate solution, the solution turns blue and silver crystals form. Why does this reaction occur?
visual_description: A copper wire immersed in a colorless silver nitrate solution gradually becomes coated with silver crystals while the solution turns blue due to Cu\u00b2\u207a ions.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: Why is potassium permanganate \((\mathrm{KMnO_4})\) in acidic solution a strong oxidizing agent?
visual_description: The tetrahedral permanganate ion (MnO\u2084\u207b) with a central manganese atom bonded to four oxygen atoms. Manganese is in the +7 oxidation state.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: On a concentration versus time graph for a reaction, how is the instantaneous rate at a specific time \(t\) determined?
visual_description: A graph of concentration (y-axis) versus time (x-axis) showing a decreasing curve for reactant concentration. A straight tangent line touches the curve at t = 2 seconds, with a negative slope indicating the rate.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: What term describes the minimum amount of energy required to break bonds in reactants so that a chemical reaction can occur?
visual_description: A graph of potential energy (y-axis) versus reaction progress (x-axis) showing a curve rising from reactants to a peak (activation energy) then descending to products. The height of the peak is labeled \(E_a\).
subject: Chemistry

question_text: Even when reactant molecules collide with sufficient energy, a reaction may still not occur. What additional condition must be satisfied?
visual_description: Two diagrams side by side: left shows NO and O₃ colliding with correct orientation (N atom of NO approaching O atom of O₃). Right shows incorrect orientation (O of NO approaching O of O₃) leading to no reaction.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process is called a:
visual_description: Reaction energy diagram showing two curves: one with higher activation energy (uncatalyzed) and one with lower activation energy (catalyzed). Reactants and products at same energy levels.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: A given mass of zinc powder reacts with hydrochloric acid much faster than an equal mass of zinc metal strips. This difference is primarily due to the powder having:
visual_description: Two diagrams: left shows a large solid cube with a small number of surface particles exposed; right shows the same mass divided into many small cubes with a much larger total surface area (highlighted).
subject: Chemistry

question_text: A catalyst increases the rate of a chemical reaction by:
visual_description: Reaction energy diagram showing uncatalyzed pathway with high activation energy peak and catalyzed pathway with lower peak. Reactants and products at same energy levels.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: The rate of a reaction between two gases increases when the concentration of one reactant is doubled. This occurs primarily because:
visual_description: Graph showing concentration vs time for two experiments: high initial concentration (steep slope, fast) and low initial concentration (shallow slope, slow).
subject: Chemistry

question_text: Increasing the temperature of a reaction mixture increases the reaction rate primarily because:
visual_description: Graph of number of molecules (y-axis) vs kinetic energy (x-axis) showing two curves: low T (narrow, tall peak) and high T (broad, lower peak). Shaded area beyond E_a is larger at high T.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: The structure of a substance refers to the arrangement of its parts. Which of the following best describes the structure of table salt (\(\mathrm{NaCl}\))?
visual_description: A three-dimensional cubic lattice showing alternating green (sodium ions) and blue (chloride ions) spheres arranged in a repeating pattern. Each sodium ion is surrounded by six chloride ions.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: Organic chemistry is the study of substances containing carbon. Which of the following is an organic compound?
visual_description: A six-carbon ring structure (hexose) with hydroxyl groups (-OH) attached to carbons 1-4 and 6, and a CH₂OH group at carbon 5.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: In Rutherford's gold foil experiment, a very small fraction of alpha particles were deflected backward. What does this observation indicate about the structure of the atom?
visual_description: A diagram showing most alpha particles passing straight through gold foil, a few deflecting at angles, and very few bouncing straight back from the nucleus.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: How does Thomson's 'plum pudding' model of the atom represent the arrangement of positive charge and electrons?
visual_description: A large sphere representing uniform positive charge with small dark circles (electrons) scattered randomly throughout the interior.
subject: Chemistry

question_text: For the graph of \(f(x) = a^x\) with \(a > 1\), what is the horizontal asymptote?
visual_description: Graph of y = 2^x showing the curve approaching the x-axis (y=0) as x decreases.

question_text: What is the vertical asymptote of the logarithmic function \(f(x) = \log_a x\) with \(a > 1\)?

question_text: The graph of \(y = \log_2 x\) is the reflection of \(y = 2^x\) across which line?

question_text: A circle has center \(O\) and chord \(PQ\) with midpoint \(M\). What is the relationship between line \(OM\) and chord \(PQ\)?

question_text: In the head-to-tail (triangle) method of vector addition, where is the tail of the second vector placed?

question_text: In the parallelogram method, where are the tails of the two vectors placed?

question_text: A displacement vector \(\vec{S} = 35\mathrm{km}\) toward 60° north of east. What is the magnitude and direction of \(-1.2\vec{S}\)?
visual_description: Vector S at 60° NE. Vector -1.2S longer and pointing opposite (60° SW).
subject: Physics

question_text: The scalar (dot) product of two vectors is defined as \(\vec{A}\cdot\vec{B} = AB\cos\theta\), where \(\theta\) is the angle between them. What is the dot product of two perpendicular vectors?
visual_description: Two perpendicular arrows: one horizontal right, one vertical up. Angle = 90°.
subject: Physics

question_text: A student incorrectly states that if \(\vec{A}\cdot\vec{B} = 0\), then at least one of the vectors must be zero. Which reasoning corrects this misconception?
visual_description: Two perpendicular nonzero vectors (horizontal right, vertical up). Angle = 90°.
subject: Physics

question_text: Collinear vectors are vectors that lie along the same line or along parallel lines. Which of the following statements about collinear vectors is correct?
visual_description: Three vectors on parallel horizontal lines: one right, one left, one right with different length.
subject: Physics

question_text: Three or more vectors lying in the same plane or parallel to the same plane are called coplanar vectors. Which set of vectors is coplanar?
visual_description: Three vectors drawn on a flat rectangular plane surface.
subject: Physics

question_text: Orthogonal vectors are vectors that are perpendicular to each other. Which condition must orthogonal vectors satisfy?
visual_description: Two perpendicular arrows: one horizontal right, one vertical up.
subject: Physics

question_text: A unit vector is a vector that has a magnitude of exactly 1 and has no units. Which statement about unit vectors is true?
visual_description: Original vector A and its unit vector u in same direction, u shorter (length 1).
subject: Physics

question_text: What is the result of adding a vector to the negative of itself?
visual_description: Two arrows of equal length: one right, one left, starting from same point. No net arrow.
subject: Physics

question_text: From Example 2.6: Vector \(\vec{A}\) has magnitude 100 m at 50° above +x axis. Vector \(\vec{B}\) has magnitude 200 m at 30° above -x axis (150° from +x). What is the magnitude of the resultant vector \(R\)?
visual_description: Vector A at 50°, vector B at 150°, resultant R from origin to (Rx,Ry).
subject: Physics

question_text: From Example 2.7, a girl walks 72 m at 32° East of North, then 57 m at 37° South of West, then 18 m South. Using the component table, what is the x-component (east/west) of the resultant displacement?
visual_description: Table with rows S1, S2, S3 and columns for x-component (m) and y-component (m).
subject: Physics

question_text: Under what condition does the vector sum of two non-zero vectors become a null (zero) vector?
visual_description: Two arrows of equal length pointing opposite directions from same point. No resultant.
subject: Physics

question_text: A vector is represented graphically by an arrow. What does the length of the arrow represent, and what does the arrowhead indicate?
visual_description: An arrow with labeled parts: length (magnitude) and arrowhead (direction).
subject: Physics

question_text: In graphical vector addition, vectors are placed head to tail. What does the resultant vector represent in this method?
visual_description: Three vectors placed head to tail, resultant arrow from start of first to end of last.
subject: Physics

question_text: A displacement vector is given as \(\vec{S} = 60\mathrm{km}\) toward North. What is the negative of this vector?
visual_description: Two arrows: one pointing up (North), one pointing down (South), same length.
subject: Physics

question_text: Under what circumstance would a vector have components that are equal in magnitude?
visual_description: Vector at 45° with equal x and y projections (both same length).
subject: Physics

question_text: A vector lies in the xy-plane. For what orientation will both of its components be negative?
visual_description: Four arrows in four quadrants with component signs labeled.
subject: Physics

question_text: On a pressure–temperature phase diagram, what does the point where the solid, liquid, and gas phases coexist in equilibrium represent?
visual_description: Pressure vs temperature graph showing solid, liquid, gas regions, triple point, and critical point.
subject: Physics

question_text: A bimetallic strip consists of brass and iron bonded together. When heated, the strip bends. Why does it bend?
visual_description: Two strips of different metals bonded together. When heated, the strip curves toward the metal with smaller expansion coefficient.
subject: Physics

question_text: Three point charges are placed on a line: \(q_1 = +3\ \mu\mathrm{C}\), \(q_2 = -2\ \mu\mathrm{C}\), \(q_3 = +1\ \mu\mathrm{C}\). The distance between adjacent charges is \(0.1\ \mathrm{m}\). What is the net force on \(q_2\) due to the other two charges? (\(k = 9.0\times10^9\ \mathrm{Nm^2/C^2}\))
visual_description: Three charges on x-axis: q1 at x=0, q2 at x=0.1 m, q3 at x=0.2 m. Forces on q2: attractive left toward q1 (5.4 N), attractive right toward q3 (1.8 N).
subject: Physics

question_text: A point charge \(Q = -4.0\ \mathrm{nC}\) produces an electric field. At a distance of \(0.02\ \mathrm{m}\) from the charge, what is the direction of the electric field?
visual_description: A negative point charge with arrows pointing inward toward the charge.
subject: Physics

question_text: How should a voltmeter be connected to measure the potential difference across a resistor?
visual_description: Circuit diagram showing voltmeter in parallel with a resistor.
subject: Physics

question_text: Four point masses, each \( m = 2.0 \, \text{kg} \), are placed at the corners of a square of side \( L = 1.0 \, \text{m} \). The square rotates about an axis through its center perpendicular to its plane. What is the moment of inertia? (Each mass is at distance \( r = L/\sqrt{2} \) from the center.)
visual_description: A square of side 1.0 m with point masses of 2.0 kg at each corner. Axis of rotation passes through the center perpendicular to the plane.
subject: Physics

question_text: A hydraulic lift has a small piston area of \(0.0040\,\text{m}^2\) and a large piston area of \(0.20\,\text{m}^2\). What force on the small piston is required to lift a \(1200\,\text{kg}\) car on the large piston?
visual_description: Two pistons connected by a fluid-filled tube. Small piston on left has area 0.0040 m², large piston on right has area 0.20 m² with a car above it.
subject: Physics

question_text: A scuba diver descends from the surface to a depth of \(15\,\text{m}\) in fresh water (\(\rho = 1000\,\text{kg/m}^3\)). By how much does the absolute pressure on the diver increase?
visual_description: Graph showing linear increase of pressure with depth: slope = ρg = 9800 Pa/m.
subject: Physics

question_text: What does a barometer directly measure by balancing the weight of a fluid column against atmospheric pressure?
visual_description: A glass tube inverted in a mercury reservoir; mercury column height around 760 mm at sea level.
subject: Physics

question_text: Why does a submerged object experience a net upward buoyant force?
visual_description: A rectangular block submerged in fluid; arrows: longer upward arrow on bottom, shorter downward arrow on top.
subject: Physics

question_text: A wooden block floats in water with \(75\%\) of its volume submerged. What is the density of the wood?
visual_description: A rectangular block floating in water; 75% of its volume below waterline, 25% above.
subject: Physics

question_text: A gas occupies \(2.0\,\text{m}^3\) at \(300\,\text{K}\) and \(150\,\text{kPa}\). If the temperature increases to \(450\,\text{K}\) at constant pressure, what is the new volume?
visual_description: Linear graph: volume (m³) on y-axis, temperature (K) on x-axis; line through (300,2.0) and (450,3.0).
subject: Physics

question_text: An open manometer has a fluid column height difference of \(0.25\,\text{m}\) with the gas side lower. The fluid density is \(850\,\text{kg/m}^3\) and atmospheric pressure is \(101.3\,\text{kPa}\). What is the absolute gas pressure?
visual_description: U-tube with fluid; left arm connected to gas, right arm open to air; gas side lower by height h.
subject: Physics

question_text: Water flows through a pipe that narrows from \(0.10\,\text{m}^2\) to \(0.02\,\text{m}^2\). If the speed in the wider section is \(1.5\,\text{m/s}\), what is the speed in the narrower section?
visual_description: Pipe wide on left (A₁=0.10 m², v₁=1.5 m/s), narrow on right (A₂=0.02 m², v₂=7.5 m/s).
subject: Physics

question_text: What characterizes steady (laminar) fluid flow?
visual_description: Left: parallel smooth streamlines (laminar). Right: chaotic, swirling paths (turbulent).
subject: Physics

question_text: An incompressible fluid flows through a pipe that widens from diameter \(d\) to \(2d\). How does the flow speed change?
visual_description: Pipe narrow left, wide right; diameter ratio 1:2; area ratio 1:4; speed ratio 4:1.
subject: Physics

question_text: An open manometer has the gas side higher than the open side by \(0.12\,\text{m}\) of a liquid with density \(900\,\text{kg/m}^3\). Atmospheric pressure is \(101\,\text{kPa}\). What is the absolute gas pressure?
visual_description: U-tube; gas connected arm has higher fluid level than open arm; height difference h.
subject: Physics

question_text: A closed-end manometer has a mercury column height of 150 mm. What is the absolute gas pressure in kPa? (ρ_Hg = 13,600 kg/m³)
visual_description: U-tube with one arm sealed and evacuated; other arm connected to gas; mercury column height h.
subject: Physics

question_text: A long straight wire carries a current toward the east. A compass is placed directly north of the wire. Which direction will the compass needle point?
visual_description: A straight wire with current arrow pointing east. A compass north of the wire shows needle pointing west.
subject: Physics

question_text: What physical quantity measures the total magnetic field lines passing through a given area?
visual_description: A rectangular loop of area A placed in a uniform magnetic field B. The angle θ between B and the area normal vector is shown.
subject: Physics

question_text: When a north pole of a bar magnet approaches a closed coil, an induced current flows. According to Lenz's law, what magnetic pole does the approaching face of the coil acquire?
visual_description: A bar magnet with north pole approaching a coil. The coil's face is labeled N (induced north pole), with field lines opposing the magnet.
subject: Physics

question_text: In an electric bell, what causes the hammer to strike the bell repeatedly?
visual_description: Diagram showing electromagnet, hammer, spring, screw contact, and bell. Hammer attracted to electromagnet, breaking contact at screw.
subject: Physics

question_text: What is the function of the commutator in a DC electric motor?
visual_description: A coil with commutator segments connected to brushes. As the coil rotates, the commutator swaps brush contact.
subject: Physics

question_text: In a reed relay, what causes the two nickel-iron blades to make electrical contact?
visual_description: A coil around a glass envelope containing two nickel-iron blades. Magnetic field lines show opposite poles induced on blades.
subject: Physics

question_text: A square loop of side \(0.10\,\mathrm{m}\) is placed in a uniform magnetic field of \(0.20\,\mathrm{T}\) such that the plane of the loop is parallel to the field. What is the magnetic flux through the loop?
visual_description: Rectangular loop oriented with its plane parallel to magnetic field lines. No field lines pass through the loop.
subject: Physics

question_text: What is a key difference between electric field lines and magnetic field lines?
visual_description: Electric field lines radiating from a positive charge to negative charge. Magnetic field lines forming closed loops around a bar magnet.
subject: Physics

question_text: On a velocity-time graph for a car moving in a straight line, the slope of the line is constant and positive. What does this indicate about the car's motion?
visual_description: A straight line with positive slope on a velocity vs time graph, indicating velocity increases uniformly with time.
subject: Physics

question_text: A cyclist rides \(5\,\mathrm{km}\) east, then \(2\,\mathrm{km}\) west. What is the magnitude of the displacement?
visual_description: A number line from -1 to 6 km. Start at 0, move to +5 (east), then back to +3 (west). Displacement is +3.
subject: Physics

question_text: On a position-time graph, a straight line with a positive slope indicates:
visual_description: A straight line with positive slope on a position vs time graph, indicating constant positive velocity.
subject: Physics

question_text: Three students start at the same point. Student A moves 200 m right then 100 m left. Student B moves 200 m right then 300 m left. Student C moves 200 m right then 200 m left. Which student has zero displacement?
visual_description: Number line showing three paths: A ends at +100, B at −100, C at 0.
subject: Physics

question_text: Between which two points on a speed-time graph is a car experiencing uniform motion?
visual_description: A horizontal line on a speed-time graph, indicating constant speed over time.
subject: Physics

question_text: What physical quantity is represented by the area under a velocity-time graph?
visual_description: A rectangle on a velocity-time graph with area = v × t, representing displacement.
